revision.text:
'''末羅国造'''（まつらのくにのみやつこ、まつらこくそう）とは、[[末羅国]]（現在の[[長崎県]][[松浦]]、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]]、[[伊万里市]]、東松浦郡）を支配した国造。[[穂積氏]]とは同祖の関係とされる。
==概要== [[国造本紀]]によると、[[成務天皇]]の時代、穂積臣同祖の伊香賀色雄の子・[[大水口宿禰|大水口足尼]]の孫にあたる[[矢田稲吉]]（やたいなぎ）を[[国造]]に定めたことに始まるとされる。末羅国は、[[魏志倭人伝]]に記される「[[末ら国]]」と考えられており、[[古事記]]・[[先代旧事本紀]]などには末羅県・末羅国造等と名が記される。矢田稲吉は[[肥前風土記]]で唐津地方の[[土蜘蛛]]討滅した大屋田子（日下部君の祖）と同一人物とする説もある。
==関連項目== * [[末羅国]] * [[穂積氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:まつらのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:長崎県の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県の歴史]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''末羅国造'''（まつらのくにのみやつこ、まつらこくそう）とは、[[末羅国]]（現在の[[長崎県]][[松浦]]、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]]、[[伊万里市]]、東松浦郡）を[[古代]]に支配した[[国造]]。[[穂積氏]]とは同祖の関係とされる。
== 概要 == *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」によると、[[成務天皇]]の時代、[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]同祖の[[大水口宿禰|大水口足尼]]の孫・'''[[矢田稲吉]]'''（やたいなぎ）を[[国造]]に定めたことに始まるとされる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 本拠 == 不明。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''末羅国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[肥前国]]'''[[松浦郡]]'''現在の[[長崎県]][[松浦]]、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]]、[[伊万里市]]、[[東松浦郡]]に相当する。
'''末羅国'''の名は、古く『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に記される「[[末ら国]]」と見えてており、『[[古事記]]』・『[[先代旧事本紀]]』などには末羅県・末羅国造等と名が記されるが、末ら国のその後は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[末羅国]] * [[穂積氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:まつらのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:長崎県の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県の歴史]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''末羅国造'''（まつらのくにのみやつこ、まつらこくぞう）とは、[[#支配領域|末羅国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」によると、[[成務天皇]]の時代、[[穂積氏|穂積臣]]同祖の[[大水口宿禰|大水口足尼]]の孫・'''[[矢田稲吉]]'''（やたいなぎ）を[[国造]]に定めたことに始まるとされる<ref>『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』（八木書店、2013年）p. 280。</ref>。
== 本拠 == 不明。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''末羅国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[肥前国]]'''[[松浦郡]]'''現在の[[長崎県]][[松浦]]、[[佐賀県]][[唐津市]]、[[伊万里市]]、[[東松浦郡]]に相当する。
'''末羅国'''の名は、古く『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に記される「[[末ら国]]」と見えてており、『[[古事記]]』・『[[先代旧事本紀]]』などには末羅県・末羅国造等と名が記されるが、末ら国のその後は不明である。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[末羅国]] * [[穂積氏]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:まつらのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:長崎県の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県の歴史]] [[Category:穂積氏]] [[Category:国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Related Topics == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.

=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.

It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:Completion tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

[[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force efresh]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

[[Category:11]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

Kokuzō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also! == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Sueha Kokuzō]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Hozumi clan]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Sueha Kokuzō]] [[ja:末羅国造]] [[en:Sueha Kokuzō]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sueha Kokuzō}}

'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰|12=draft}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=draft}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|ja|松浦氏|12=draft}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|ja|肥前国|12=draft}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura|ja|松浦市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Karatsu|ja|唐津市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Imari|ja|伊万里市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashi-Matsuura District|ja|東松浦郡|12=draft}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝|12=draft}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}}

<!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--><!--Sueha Kokuzō--> [[Category:Hozumi Clan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q22125226}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q22125226]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakuchi Ashinai|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The [[:en:Matsura clan|Matsura clan]] (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], corresponding to modern-day [[:en:Matsuura, Nagasaki|Matsuura]], [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], and [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]], as well as the [[:en:Higashimatsuura District, Saga|Higashi-Matsuura District]].
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]'' and is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==

The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]'' and is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]'' and is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22125226]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakuchi Ashinai|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The [[:en:Matsura clan|Matsura clan]] (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], corresponding to modern-day [[:en:Matsuura, Nagasaki|Matsuura]], [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], and [[:en:Imari, Saga|Imari]] in [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]], as well as the [[:en:Higashimatsuura District, Saga|Higashi-Matsuura District]].
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]'' and is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==

== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakuchi Ashinai|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|en|Matsura clan|WD=Q11531840|lt=Matsura clan}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura, Nagasaki|en|Matsuura, Nagasaki|WD=Q850465|lt=Matsuura}}, {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu}}, and {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashimatsuura District, Saga|en|Higashimatsuura District, Saga|WD=Q1043844|lt=Higashi-Matsuura District}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also == * [[Matsura Province]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|末羅国造|version=104168050|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:末羅国造]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:末羅国造]]
'''Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (末羅国造, Governor of Matsura) was the title of the local ruler who governed the Matsura Province.
== Overview == According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, the grandson of {{ill|Ōminakuchi Ashinai|ja|大水口宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakuchi Ashinai|1=Oomizuguchi no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436785}}, named '''{{ill|Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja|矢田稲吉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Ineyoshi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (やたのいなぎ), was appointed as the ''Kuni no Miyatsuko'' of Matsura.<ref>''Research on the Kokuzō System - Historical Materials and Analysis'' (Yagi Shoten, 2013), p. 280.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Matsura clan|en|Matsura clan|WD=Q11531840|lt=Matsura clan|1=Matsura clan}} (surname: ''Na'') is traditionally associated with the governance of this region.
== Base of Operations == The precise location of their administrative base is unknown.
=== Jurisdiction === The Matsura no Kuni no Miyatsuko's jurisdiction encompassed the area known as Matsura Province, which later became part of {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}}, corresponding to modern-day {{ill|Matsuura, Nagasaki|en|Matsuura, Nagasaki|WD=Q850465|lt=Matsuura|1=Matsuura, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Karatsu, Saga|en|Karatsu, Saga|WD=Q738275|lt=Karatsu|1=Karatsu, Saga}}, and {{ill|Imari, Saga|en|Imari, Saga|WD=Q857266|lt=Imari|1=Imari, Saga}} in {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}, as well as the {{ill|Higashimatsuura District, Saga|en|Higashimatsuura District, Saga|WD=Q1043844|lt=Higashi-Matsuura District|1=Higashimatsuura District, Saga}}.
The name ''Matsura'' is one of the oldest regional names in Japan. It appears as "Matsurokoku" in the ''{{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei|1=Wajinden}}'' and is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''. However, the history of Matsura Province beyond these early references remains unclear.
